title: medicago sativa f. glutinosa
intro: Medicago sativa f. glutinosa, also known as the 'Lucerne' or 'Bigflower lucerne', is a subspecies of the Medicago sativa plant, belonging to the family Fabaceae and commonly used as a fodder crop. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to stimulate the digestive system and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Medicago sativa f. glutinosa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support bone health, making it a valuable addition to a healthy diet. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as a tea, tincture, or powder, and can also be added to soups and stews for its nutritional benefits.
